Portfolio
About How I Work Case Studies
{/* ── Hero / About ── */}
UI/UX Design Student · University of Ottawa

Hi, I'm Your Name

I'm a Computer Science student passionate about combining artistic design and web development to create digital experiences that are both visually engaging and intuitive. Through my UI/UX coursework at the University of Ottawa, I'm exploring user-centred design, visual communication, interaction design, and modern web technologies to build experiences that are both creative and functional.

User Research Prototyping Visual Design Interaction Design
{/* ── How I Work ── */}

How I Work

I'm currently enrolled in a UI Design course at the University of Ottawa, where I'm building a foundation in evidence-based design practice.

01

Research & Understand

I start by understanding the problem space — who the users are, what they need, and what context they're in. I draw on resources like the Nielsen Norman Group for research methods.

02

Explore & Ideate

I sketch ideas rapidly, explore multiple directions, and apply visual communication principles — hierarchy, contrast, alignment, and proximity — to guide design decisions.

03

Build & Iterate

Using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, I translate designs into working prototypes and refine them based on feedback. I'm learning to treat design as an iterative, not final, process.

{/* ── Case Studies ── */}

Case Studies

Four projects completed throughout the semester. Each explores a different design challenge and context.
